problem_statement: Mullu Control Plane has multiple mature planning, simulation, execution, closure, and learning lineages, but it lacks one canonical reference-only profile that proves how those lineages interoperate without introducing a second planner or execution spine.
success_criteria: GovernedPlanningProfile validates against a JSON schema and Foundation Mode example

the profile binds the existing ProblemStar, Phi-GPS, planning compiler, simulator, capability plan, plan executor, plan ledger, OrgOS, WHQR/MIL, holistic loop, UAO, and learning-admission lineages by reference

the profile represents goals, constraints, evidence, assumptions, unknowns, contradictions, candidate plans, risk, budget, authority, approvals, rollback, compensation, safe stops, closure, and learning refs
the profile requires local repair first, hysteresis, cooldown, change budgets, and Phi_gov review for goal, policy, or authority changes

the profile keeps planner replacement, parallel execution spines, execution, dispatch, connector calls, writes, system-of-record migration, runtime replanning, success claims, and terminal closure denied
focused tests reject authority drift, source-binding drift, missing planning refs, adaptation drift, malformed payloads, missing repository paths, and summary drift
non_goals: do not register a new planner
do not replace Phi-GPS, WHQR/MIL, OrgOS, Workflow, UAO, or Phi_gov
do not bind the simulation goal compiler into router or live execution paths
do not dispatch workers or call connectors
do not write files, memory, policy, or system-of-record state
do not enable autonomous replanning or grant terminal closure
constraints: the first slice is additive, deterministic, read-only, and reversible
all source lineages preserve their existing identities and authority rules
missing adapter, shadow, runtime, or operator evidence remains AwaitingEvidence
evidence_required: focused positive and negative pytest coverage
